title: A Country Pathway.
content:
В В В В В В В В I come upon it suddenly, alone - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В A little pathway winding in the weeds
В В В В В В В В That fringe the roadside; and with dreams my own,
В В В В В В В В В В В В I wander as it leads.

В В В В В В В В Full wistfully along the slender way,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Through summer tan of freckled shade and shine,
В В В В В В В В I take the path that leads me as it may - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Its every choice is mine.

В В В В В В В В A chipmunk, or a sudden-whirring quail,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Is startled by my step as on I fare - 
В В В В В В В В A garter-snake across the dusty trail
В В В В В В В В В В В В Glances and - is not there.

В В В В В В В В Above the arching jimson-weeds flare twos
В В В В В В В В В В В В And twos of sallow-yellow butterflies,
В В В В В В В В Like blooms of lorn primroses blowing loose
В В В В В В В В В В В В When autumn winds arise.

В В В В В В В В The trail dips - dwindles - broadens then, and lifts
В В В В В В В В В В В В Itself astride a cross-road dubiously,
В В В В В В В В And, from the fennel marge beyond it, drifts
В В В В В В В В В В В В Still onward, beckoning me.

В В В В В В В В And though it needs must lure me mile on mile
В В В В В В В В В В В В Out of the public highway, still I go,
В В В В В В В В My thoughts, far in advance in Indian-file,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Allure me even so.

В В В В В В В В Why, I am as a long-lost boy that went
В В В В В В В В В В В В At dusk to bring the cattle to the bars,
В В В В В В В В And was not found again, though Heaven lent
В В В В В В В В В В В В His mother ail the stars

В В В В В В В В With which to seek him through that awful night.
В В В В В В В В В В В В O years of nights as vain! - Stars never rise
В В В В В В В В But well might miss their glitter in the light
В В В В В В В В В В В В Of tears in mother-eyes!

В В В В В В В В So - on, with quickened breaths, I follow still - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В My avant-courier must be obeyed!
В В В В В В В В Thus am I led, and thus the path, at will,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Invites me to invade

В В В В В В В В A meadow's precincts, where my daring guide
В В В В В В В В В В В В Clambers the steps of an old-fashioned stile,
В В В В В В В В And stumbles down again, the other side,
В В В В В В В В В В В В To gambol there awhile

В В В В В В В В In pranks of hide-and-seek, as on ahead
В В В В В В В В В В В В I see it running, while the clover-stalks
В В В В В В В В Shake rosy fists at me, as though they said - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В "You dog our country-walks

В В В В В В В В And mutilate us with your walking-stick! - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В We will not suffer tamely what you do
В В В В В В В В And warn you at your peril, - for we'll sic
В В В В В В В В В В В В Our bumble-bees on you!"

В В В В В В В В But I smile back, in airy nonchalance, - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В The more determined on my wayward quest,
В В В В В В В В As some bright memory a moment dawns
В В В В В В В В В В В В A morning in my breast - 

В В В В В В В В Sending a thrill that hurries me along
В В В В В В В В В В В В In faulty similes of childish skips,
В В В В В В В В Enthused with lithe contortions of a song
В В В В В В В В В В В В Performing on my lips.

В В В В В В В В In wild meanderings o'er pasture wealth - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Erratic wanderings through dead'ning-lands,
В В В В В В В В Where sly old brambles, plucking me by stealth,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Put berries in my hands:

В В В В В В В В Or, the path climbs a boulder - wades a slough - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Or, rollicking through buttercups and flags,
В В В В В В В В Goes gaily dancing o'er a deep bayou
В В В В В В В В В В В В On old tree-trunks and snags:

В В В В В В В В Or, at the creek, leads o'er a limpid pool
В В В В В В В В В В В В Upon a bridge the stream itself has made,
В В В В В В В В With some Spring-freshet for the mighty tool
В В В В В В В В В В В В That its foundation laid.

В В В В В В В В I pause a moment here to bend and muse,
В В В В В В В В В В В В With dreamy eyes, on my reflection, where
В В В В В В В В A boat-backed bug drifts on a helpless cruise,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Or wildly oars the air,

В В В В В В В В As, dimly seen, the pirate of the brook - 
В В В В В В В В В В В В The pike, whose jaunty hulk denotes his speed - 
В В В В В В В В Swings pivoting about, with wary look
В В В В В В В В В В В В Of low and cunning greed.

В В В В В В В В Till, filled with other thought, I turn again
В В В В В В В В В В В В To where the pathway enters in a realm
В В В В В В В В Of lordly woodland, under sovereign reign
В В В В В В В В В В В В Of towering oak and elm.

В В В В В В В В A puritanic quiet here reviles
В В В В В В В В В В В В The almost whispered warble from the hedge,
В В В В В В В В And takes a locust's rasping voice and files
В В В В В В В В В В В В The silence to an edge.

В В В В В В В В In such a solitude my somber way
В В В В В В В В В В В В Strays like a misanthrope within a gloom
В В В В В В В В Of his own shadows - till the perfect day
В В В В В В В В В В В В Bursts into sudden bloom,

В В В В В В В В And crowns a long, declining stretch of space,
В В В В В В В В В В В В Where King Corn's armies lie with flags unfurled,
В В В В В В В В And where the valley's dint in Nature's face
В В В В В В В В В В В В Dimples a smiling world.

В В В В В В В В And lo! through mists that may not be dispelled,
В В В В В В В В В В В В I see an old farm homestead, as in dreams,
В В В В В В В В Where, like a gem in costly setting held,
В В В В В В В В В В В В The old log cabin gleams.

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В В В В В *В В В В В В В В В В В В *

В В В В В В В В O darling Pathway! lead me bravely on
В В В В В В В В В В В В Adown your valley way, and run before
В В В В В В В В Among the roses crowding up the lawn
В В В В В В В В В В В В And thronging at the door, - 

В В В В В В В В And carry up the echo there that shall
В В В В В В В В В В В В Arouse the drowsy dog, that he may bay
В В В В В В В В The household out to greet the prodigal
В В В В В В В В В В В В That wanders home to-day.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Whitcomb Riley represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of James Whitcomb Riley's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
